feature: Compact TaskGraph text summary + per-signal phase timing telemetry
summary: Two additive observability improvements: (1) TaskGraph gains a compact plain-text summary (to_summary_text()) listing task counts, batching opportunities, and top-level constraints — useful for agent briefings and CLI output without full JSON serialisation. (2) SignalPhase now records per-signal wall-clock durations in phase_timings.per_signal (keyed by signal type, in addition to the aggregate signals_seconds); JSON output exposes the nested map and Rich output renders top-slow-signal hints when verbosity is enabled. Both additions are purely additive with no signal, scoring, or breaking API changes.
design_goals: Give agents a token-efficient text representation of a TaskGraph without JSON overhead
Surface per-signal timing so slow signals can be identified without profiling tools
Keep both additions non-breaking and backwards-compatible
non_goals: No changes to scoring weights or thresholds
No breaking changes to TaskGraph or pipeline API

self_analysis.notes: No signal logic changed. Both features are additive observability improvements.
